paper_metadata.title: Measurement of Binding Constant of Cyclodextrin by Ultrafiltration
paper_metadata.journal: Journal of Chemical Engineering of Japan

receptors.name: alpha-cyclodextrin (alpha-CD)
receptors.structure_notes: Cyclic oligosaccharide of six alpha-1,4-linked D-glucopyranose units; truncated cone cavity; optically active D-form host.
receptors.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; formula C36H60O30 matches alpha-CD; 30 chiral centers with CIP pattern consistent with six alpha-1,4-linked D-glucopyranose units (per unit: anomeric C1=S, C2/C3/C5=R, C4=R/S as expected).

receptors.name: beta-cyclodextrin (beta-CD)
receptors.structure_notes: Cyclic oligosaccharide of seven alpha-1,4-linked D-glucopyranose units; larger cavity than alpha-CD; optically active D-form host.
receptors.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; formula C42H70O35 matches beta-CD; 35 chiral centers with CIP pattern consistent with seven alpha-1,4-linked D-glucopyranose units.

analytes.name: o-nitrophenol (o-NP)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C6H5NO3; ortho substitution confirmed.

analytes.name: m-nitrophenol (m-NP)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C6H5NO3; meta substitution confirmed.

analytes.name: p-nitrophenol (p-NP)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C6H5NO3; para substitution confirmed.

analytes.name: L-threonine (L-Thr)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C4H9NO3; CIP (2S,3R) matches L-threonine.

analytes.name: D-threonine (D-Thr)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C4H9NO3; CIP (2R,3S) matches D-threonine (enantiomer of L-Thr).

analytes.name: L-phenylalanine (L-Phe)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C9H11NO2; CIP alpha-C = S matches L-phenylalanine.

analytes.name: D-phenylalanine (D-Phe)
analytes.smiles_verification_notes: RDKit parses; C9H11NO2; CIP alpha-C = R matches D-phenylalanine (enantiomer of L-Phe).

conditions.method: ultrafiltration-based binding constant measurement; [CD]0 = 5 mM, [G]0 = 2.5-10 mM; equilibrium K_B obtained at the plateau region where 1/dP > 1/dP_B (low pressure plateau)
conditions.notes: K_B at plateau is the equilibrium binding constant; values read from Figs. 2, 3 and 9, 10 at pH 7.

evidence.quote: K_B of p-NP is about 5 times larger than those of o- and m-NPs ... for alpha-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Plateau K_B values read from Fig. 2 for alpha-CD at pH 7.

evidence.quote: K_B of p-NP is about 5 times larger than those of o- and m-NPs

pairwise_labels.notes: Plateau region of Fig. 2; p-NP fits the smaller alpha-CD cavity best.

evidence.quote: o- and m-NPs ... are more structurally stable in beta-CD than in alpha-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Read from Fig. 3 plateau (1/dP > 1) for beta-CD at pH 7.

evidence.quote: o- and m-NPs ... are more structurally stable in beta-CD than in alpha-CD ... because of the steric hindrance effect

pairwise_labels.notes: Plateau region of Fig. 3 at pH 7.

evidence.quote: Plateau K_B values in Fig. 3 for beta-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Both values close (~700 vs ~600); ordering visible in Fig. 3 plateau.

pairwise_labels.normalized_direction: larger K_B = stronger binding for given guest

evidence.quote: the inner diameter of the cavity of alpha-CD was so large that a benzene ring could just fit in. ... p-NP might thus just fit in to the alpha-CD cavity

pairwise_labels.notes: Receptor selectivity comparison: alpha-CD vs beta-CD for p-NP. Treated as receptor comparison (target = alpha-CD-p-NP, interferent = beta-CD-p-NP).

evidence.quote: the optically active D-form CD tends to include optically heterogeneous L-Thr rather than homogeneous D-Thr under the present conditions

pairwise_labels.notes: Read from Fig. 9 at pH 7 for alpha-CD.

evidence.quote: CD tended to include optical L-acid rather than D-acid under the present conditions

pairwise_labels.notes: Read from Fig. 9 at pH 7 for beta-CD; values close, ordering preserved.

evidence.quote: The D-form optical isomer CD tends to include L-Phe rather than D-Phe in a similar manner as Thr

pairwise_labels.notes: Read from Fig. 10 at pH 7 for alpha-CD.

evidence.quote: The difference of K_B between L- and D-Phes is larger at high pH. Optical resolution of Phe must be carried out ... using beta-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Read from Fig. 10 at pH 7 for beta-CD.

evidence.quote: The K_B of Phe for beta-CD is larger than that for alpha-CD, in contrast with that of Thr ... Phe is included more stable in beta-CD than in alpha-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Receptor selectivity for Phe; bulkier benzyl side chain fits beta-CD better.

evidence.quote: As the diameter of Thr molecules is smaller than that of nitrophenol (NP), the K_B of Thr for alpha-CD is structurally larger than that for beta-CD

pairwise_labels.notes: Receptor selectivity for L-Thr; smaller Thr fits alpha-CD better.
